children.name: Oreoica
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Crested Bellbird, a medium-sized, crest-bearing passerine endemic to Australia. It is named for its extraordinary, ventriloquial song that sounds like a clear, ringing bell from a distance, making its exact location difficult to pinpoint. It inhabits dry woodlands and shrublands, foraging on the ground for insects and seeds.

children.name: Oreoica gutturalis
children.description: A medium-sized, grey-brown bird with a prominent black crest and a white throat, famous for its rich, bell-like song that sounds as though it comes from a much larger bird, found across much of Australia. Its ventriloquial abilities make it difficult to locate even when its ringing call seems close at hand. It feeds on the ground in dry woodlands, hopping with its crest raised.

children.name: Ornorectes
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Crested Pitohui, a robust, thrush-sized passerine endemic to the lowland forests of New Guinea. It is one of the few known poisonous bird genera, with its skin and feathers containing batrachotoxin, the same toxin found in poison dart frogs. It has a distinctive bright orange crest that it raises when excited.

children.name: Ornorectes cristatus
children.description: A sturdy, thrush-like bird with a bright orange crown and crest, olive-brown upperparts, and a powerful bill, found in the lowland and hill forests of New Guinea. Like other pitohuis, its skin and feathers contain a potent neurotoxin that deters predators. It forages for fruits and insects in the understorey and mid-storey, often joining mixed-species flocks.

children.name: Pseudorectes
children.description: A genus of moderate-sized, thrush-like passerines endemic to New Guinea, formerly placed with the whistlers but now recognised as part of the bellbird family. They have robust bodies, strong legs, and a stocky build adapted for a largely terrestrial or near-ground foraging lifestyle. Their plumage is predominantly brown and rufous with subtle barring.

children.name: Pseudorectes ferrugineus
children.description: A medium-sized, thrush-like bird with rich rufous-brown upperparts, a bright yellow belly, and a stout black bill, found in the lowland and hill forests of New Guinea. It forages methodically in the lower storey, feeding on fruit and large insects, often in pairs or small family groups. Its simple, piping whistle carries through the forest understorey.
